summary: Customer discovery notes reveal a direct process conflict between Marketing and Sales regarding the lead qualification and routing workflow. Marketing expects automated qualification to maximize speed, while Sales requires a mandatory manual approval gate for every lead. No project decision owner has been assigned to resolve this, leading to process ambiguity and potential CRM data pollution.
topBlockers: Conflict between automatic lead qualification (Marketing) and manual lead approval (Sales).
Missing decision owner to resolve lead-routing and CRM entry policy.
Lack of defined success metrics to balance lead velocity against lead quality.

risks.title: Conflicting lead-approval requirements

evidence.excerpt: Marketing wants automatic lead qualification based on web activity. However, Sales demands a manual review of every qualified lead...

risks.implementationImpact: The lead-routing system cannot be configured. Proceeding without resolution will cause Sales reps to ignore automatically qualified leads or create severe friction between departments.
risks.requiredClarificationOrAction: Establish a unified lead definition workshop. Decide on an automated scoring threshold with a manual override protocol.
risks.confidenceReason: The conflict between Sales and Marketing is explicitly stated in discovery notes.

risks.title: Missing ownership for workflow mediation

evidence.excerpt: No project owner has been assigned to mediate this dispute.

risks.implementationImpact: Project timeline drift. Decisions will be delayed indefinitely because there is no designated owner to finalize functional requirements.
risks.requiredClarificationOrAction: Formally assign a project sponsor or executive owner from Operations to sign off on cross-departmental rules.
risks.suggestedBlockerReason: No owner is assigned to mediate.

risks.title: Undefined lead-routing success metrics

evidence.excerpt: Marketing wants automatic lead qualification... Sales demands manual review...

risks.implementationImpact: Inability to measure rollout success or ROI. The team will argue about qualification rates versus lead conversion rates without objective baselines.
risks.requiredClarificationOrAction: Define baseline metrics: Target conversion rate (>15%) and lead processing SLA (<2 hours).

stakeholders.name: Marketing Team
goals: Automate lead transition into the CRM immediately
conflicts: Requires automated qualification which Sales opposes
stakeholders.authority: Can configure top-of-funnel capture but does not own CRM lead status logic

stakeholders.name: Sales Team
goals: Filter out low-intent prospects before manual follow-up
conflicts: Requires manual approval for every lead, slowing down the pipeline
stakeholders.authority: Owns bottom-of-funnel pipeline and can refuse to work leads

dependencies.name: Marketing Automation API
dependencies.description: Integration and syncing of web activity data to the CRM for scoring.

dependencies.name: Lead Assignment Rules Configuration
dependencies.description: Functional mapping of lead ownership in CRM based on geography and segment.

decisions.decision: Determine lead qualification model (Fully Automated vs. Manual Gate)
decisions.consequence: Delaying this will halt CRM routing development and delay launch by several weeks.

validationQuestions.question: What is the acceptable maximum delay (SLA) for a lead to sit in a manual review queue?
If Sales and Marketing cannot agree, who makes the binding decision on CRM routing rules?

rolloutRecommendations.action: Convene lead management alignment workshop with Sales and Marketing leads.
Configure hybrid scoring model (automated scoring with manual exception queue) in sandbox.
Train Sales reps on qualification tools and monitor conversion rate metrics.
